content.full_text:
Farn PoLrrrcAL PnacrrcEs CoiraMrssIoN
428 J Street. Suite 620. Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660. Fax (916) 322-0886
January 22,2003
Howard Laks Associates Architects
1545 Twelfth Street
Santa Monica, CA 90401
Your Request for Advice
Our File No. 4-02-346
Dear Mr. Laks
Re
This letter is in response to your request for advice regarding the conflict-of-
interest provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").1 Your request is specifically
in reference to the l4n 7b Street, Santa Monica, California multi-family project ("mulii-
family project").
QUESTION
May you participate, as an agent of your client, in the presentation of the multi-
family project when it is presented to the planning commission on appeal?
CONCLUSION
Based on our previous analysis and advice to you, as referenced in the two
separate advice letters listed below, you may represent your client regarding the multi-
family project before the planning commission. However, you may only act in your
private/professional capacity and may not purport to act in your official capacity as a
member of the architectural review board.
FACTS
You are a member of the City of Santa Monica Architectural Review Board
("ARB"). As a practicing architect within the City of Santa Monica, you have designed a
multi-family apartment project that was subject to the review of the architectural review
I Government Code sections 81000 - 91014. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections
18109-18997, of the California Code of Regulations.


File No. A-02-346
Page No. 2
board. You recused yourself by leaving the council chambers during the review of this
specific item when it was presented to the board. The architectural ieview board
approved the project, however, two members of the general public have appealed the
decision of the ARB to the planning commission. The planning "o--is.io, will review
the project on January 22,2003.
You recently requested and received advice from the FPPC this year in two
separate letters that advised on similar issues. (Lat<"s Advice Letters Nos. e-OZ-155 and
A-02-215.) You have specifically asked whether your culrent request is consistent with
the previous advice we have issued to you in both of these letters.
In Advice Letter No. 4-02-215, you were advised that you may appearbefore the
city planning commission in your privat e capacity, to present an appeal of an
architectural review board ruling against your client'sievelopm"rfproporul. That letter
concemed a different proposal than the multi-family project. You were also advised that
you may participate in the presentation as long as you do not purport to act in an official
capacity as an ARB member.
In Advice Letter No. 4-02-155, we advised you that the City of Santa Monica
Planning Commission is not the same agency as, or under the budgetary authority of the
ARB. Since the planning commission and the ARB are not considereda single agency,
but are two separate agencies for purposes of the Act's conflict-of-interest *'i"r,
regulation 18702.3(a) does not apply.
There is nothing in your current request that alters our previous written advice to
you. Therefore, you may represent your client regarding the multi-family project before
the planning commission. However, you may only act i, yorrr pivate/pioiessionul
capacity and may not purport to act in your official capacity as a member of the
architectural review board.
If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916)
322-5660.
Sincerely,
Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel
By: u#ktt'w
JSje

Counsel, Legal Division
